{"type":"tool_call","sequence":27,"time":"2026-05-15T00:42:56.230451173Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T004134-f2c48440","base_rev":"45be3cb39e470a40d49592f9c742a787686c8bd3","bead_id":"helix-81c0c0df","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T004134-f2c48440","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T004134-f2c48440/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"cf08fa691dd933c31a3781362ed7f6b7fd06f9ccf2c28c536e49c7172658e91a","session_id":"eb-44729bfa","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_15","name":"bash","input":{"command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,280p' docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-adapter-boundary.md\""}}}
{"type":"tool_result","sequence":28,"time":"2026-05-15T00:42:56.230621548Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T004134-f2c48440","base_rev":"45be3cb39e470a40d49592f9c742a787686c8bd3","bead_id":"helix-81c0c0df","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T004134-f2c48440","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T004134-f2c48440/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"cf08fa691dd933c31a3781362ed7f6b7fd06f9ccf2c28c536e49c7172658e91a","session_id":"eb-44729bfa","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_15","output":"---\nddx:\n  id: CONTRACT-003\n  type: contract\n  phase: design\n  depends_on:\n    - helix.prd\n    - CONTRACT-001\n    - CONTRACT-002\n  status: draft\n---\n\n# CONTRACT-003: DDx Adapter Boundary\n\n**Status:** Draft\n**Owner:** HELIX maintainers\n**Related:** [PRD](../../01-frame/prd.md) (R-4, R-7),\n[Minimal Runtime Contract](../../../docs/install/README.md#minimal-runtime-contract),\n[CONTRACT-001](CONTRACT-001-ddx-helix-boundary.md),\n[CONTRACT-002](CONTRACT-002-helix-execution-doc-conventions.md),\n[Artifact Schema](../../../workflows/artifact-schema.md)\n\n## Purpose\n\nAfter the HELIX scope collapse (2026-Q2), HELIX is methodology + artifact\ncatalog + one routing skill. DDx is no longer HELIX's platform substrate; it is\none of three target runtimes — alongside Claude Code and Databricks Code Genie.\n\nThis contract redraws the boundary in that new shape:\n\n\u003e **DDx is a runtime that adapts HELIX content for its specific surfaces. HELIX\n\u003e portable behavior ends at the minimal runtime contract; DDx-specific behavior\n\u003e begins there.**\n\nCONTRACT-001 captured the old shape, where HELIX was built on DDx and delegated\nexecution, tracking, and queue management to it. That remains a useful guide to\nshared integration objects but reads the relationship in the wrong direction\nafter the collapse. CONTRACT-003 supersedes the directional framing of\nCONTRACT-001; it does not invalidate the shared-object definitions or audit\nfindings therein.\n\n## What HELIX Provides (DDx Consumes)\n\nDDx consumes three portable content artifacts from HELIX:\n\n### 1. Artifact catalog — `workflows/`\n\nThe canonical location is `workflows/` in the HELIX repo. When installed via\n`ddx install helix`, the catalog lands under\n`.ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/phases/\u003cphase\u003e/artifacts/\u003ctype\u003e/`.\n\nEach artifact type exposes:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `meta.yml` | Artifact-type metadata per `workflows/artifact-schema.md` |\n| `template.md` | Markdown skeleton for new instances |\n| `prompt.md` | Authoring guidance for agents or humans |\n| `example.md` | Canonical illustrative instance |\n\nDDx may index these files into its doc-graph and make them discoverable for\nsearch, alignment, and bead-authoring assistance. It must not modify their\ncontent during indexing or treat its own index as the schema authority.\n\n### 2. Routing skill — `skills/helix/SKILL.md`\n\nThe single skill entry point. Its frontmatter shape is:\n\n```yaml\n---\nname: helix\ndescription: \u003crouting hint text\u003e\nargument-hint: \"[intent or scope]\"\n---\n```\n\nThree keys only; no DDx-specific fields. DDx discovers the skill via the\nstandard plugin skill loader and invokes it the same way Claude Code or Genie\nwould — by presenting the frontmatter and body to the active agent session.\nDDx must not inject DDx-specific fields into the skill frontmatter or copy the\nskill body into its own runtime store in a divergent form.\n\n### 3. Artifact-type schema — `workflows/artifact-schema.md`\n\nThe normative schema for `meta.yml` and `ddx:` instance frontmatter. DDx is the\ncurrent reference consumer (the `ddx:` namespace is historical). DDx must treat\nthis document as the schema authority. If DDx extends the schema, it must file\nchanges here and not define a parallel schema in its own docs.\n\n**What HELIX does NOT provide:** a tracker, a queue, an execution loop, a CLI,\nbead IDs, execution evidence, metrics, or runtime state. Those are DDx concerns.\n\n## What DDx Provides as Runtime\n\nDDx provides the execution layer that HELIX's content runs inside. This does not\nmake DDx authoritative over HELIX content; it makes DDx responsible for the\nfollowing surfaces:\n\n### 1. Bead tracker\n\nDDx owns bead storage (`.ddx/beads.jsonl`), bead lifecycle (open → executing →\nclosed), and queue ordering (`ReadyExecution()`). HELIX content may describe\nthe shape of a well-formed bead, but HELIX does not store, claim, or close\nbeads. DDx does.\n\n### 2. Execute-loop and dispatch\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` is DDx's queue-drain primitive. `ddx agent execute-bead`\nis the single-bead primitive. HELIX routing skill may invoke `build` or `run`\nmodes that ultimately call these, but the invocation path goes through the\nagent session, not through a HELIX-owned subprocess. DDx owns harness\nselection, model routing, and session management.\n\n### 3. Evidence and metrics\n\nDDx writes execution evidence to `.ddx/exec/runs/`. HELIX content can author\nexecution documents (`EXEC-*.md`) that describe what DDx should validate;\nDDx runs the validations and writes the results. HELIX does not write to the\nevidence store.\n\n### 4. File checkpoint and prose checker\n\n`ddx checkpoint` and `ddx doc prose` are DDx-owned. HELIX content describes\nwhen a checkpoint is appropriate and authors `validation.pattern_checks` in\n`meta.yml` in a form DDx's prose checker can enforce; DDx executes both.\n\u003c!-- TODO: verify with DDx maintainers — auto-checkpoint before execute-bead\n     tracked as ddx-91bc770a; per-artifact-type rule scoping in ddx doc prose\n     tracked as PRD R-9 — confirm DDx bead exists for the latter --\u003e\n\n### 5. Plugin packaging\n\n`ddx install helix` is DDx's plugin installer. It copies the HELIX catalog and\nskill into `.ddx/plugins/helix/`. The DDx plugin manifest (format owned by DDx)\nwraps the HELIX content. HELIX does not define the plugin manifest format;\nDDx does.\n\n## The Interface\n\n### What DDx reads from HELIX\n\n| Artifact | Location | DDx usage |\n|---|---|---|\n| Artifact-type metadata | `meta.yml` per artifact type | Doc-graph indexing, prose-checker rules |\n| Artifact templates | `template.md` per artifact type | Surface to agents for bead authoring assistance |\n| Authoring prompts | `prompt.md` per artifact type | Surface to agents during frame, design, evolve |\n| Artifact schema | `workflows/artifact-schema.md` | Authoritative field definitions for `ddx:` frontmatter |\n| Routing skill body | `skills/helix/SKILL.md` | Loaded into agent sessions via plugin skill loader |\n| Instance frontmatter | `ddx:` block in project `docs/helix/` files | `ddx.id`, `ddx.depends_on`, `ddx.type`, `ddx.status` for graph traversal and queue dep-resolution |\n\nDDx must consume these as read-only inputs. Indexing and caching are fine;\nmodifying the catalog source is not.\n\n### What DDx writes\n\n| Artifact | Location | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Beads | `.ddx/beads.jsonl` | Owned by DDx; HELIX does not write here directly |\n| Execution runs | `.ddx/exec/runs/` | DDx persists run manifests and evidence |\n| Plugin layout | `.ddx/plugins/helix/` | DDx installs HELIX content here; source stays in `workflows/` |\n| Runtime events | `.ddx/events/` or inline bead fields | DDx writes `execute-loop-*` fields; HELIX must not write these |\n| Session/transcript capture | DDx session store | DDx-owned; not part of HELIX artifact schema |\n\n### What the runtime contract requires (minimum)\n\nFrom `docs/install/README.md`, items 1–3 are required for all HELIX routes:\n\n1. Read markdown files from the project filesystem.\n2. Write markdown files to the project filesystem.\n3. Search files by path or pattern.\n\nItem 4 (shell execution) is optional; DDx satisfies it fully. DDx is therefore\na full-capability HELIX runtime. The DDx adapter may expose additional surfaces\n(bead tracker, execute-loop, metrics), but those are DDx extensions, not HELIX\nrequirements.\n\n### Execute-loop result surface (DDx → HELIX skill)\n\nWhen the HELIX routing skill's `build` or `run` modes delegate to\n`ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`, the skill reads\n`results[].bead_id` for post-cycle bookkeeping and `results[].status`\n(`success`, `no_changes`, `execution_failed`, `land_conflict`,\n`post_run_check_failed`, `structural_validation_failed`) to decide workflow\ncontinuation. Full payload shape and status semantics are defined in\nCONTRACT-001 §DDx -\u003e HELIX, which remains authoritative for this surface.\n\n## What Is NOT in Scope\n\n### HELIX must not know about\n\n- Bead IDs, bead lifecycle, or claim mechanics. HELIX content may mention\n  \"work items\" generically; it must not depend on `.ddx/beads.jsonl` format.\n- DDx internal fields (`execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-failed-routes`,\n  etc.). These must not appear in HELIX artifact templates, prompts, or skill\n  body.\n- DDx plugin manifest format. HELIX does not own or define the\n  `.ddx/plugins/helix/` layout; it only defines the source content.\n- DDx model-routing, harness selection, or power-class labels. HELIX skill may\n  request a tier constraint in `build`/`run` modes, but must not embed DDx\n  model names or harness names in its normative body.\n\n### DDx must not require\n\n- A particular execute-bead contract beyond the minimal runtime contract\n  (read-file, write-file, search-files). DDx may offer a richer contract as an\n  opt-in; it must not mandate it for HELIX content to be usable.\n- HELIX artifacts to carry DDx-specific fields as required fields. All DDx\n  operational fields in `ddx:` frontmatter must be ignorable by Claude Code and\n  Genie without changing artifact meaning (per artifact-schema §consumer\n  responsibilities and §optional extension fields).\n- HELIX to implement a queue, a tracker, or an execution loop. If DDx needs\n  queue semantics, DDx provides them.\n\n## Migration Notes: Known Boundary Leaks\n\nThe following places in the current codebase cross the new boundary. Each entry\ncarries a file reference; none is a blocking defect today but each should be\nresolved before the next HELIX major release.\n\n### LEAK-1 — Skill body references DDx-specific route names\n\n`skills/helix/SKILL.md:221`\n\n```\nPreserve DDx execute-bead history: never squash, rebase, amend, or filter\nbranches containing execute-bead or `[ddx-*]` commits.\n```\n\nThe `commit` workflow contract names `execute-bead` and `[ddx-*]` — DDx-specific\nconcepts — in the normative skill body. This violates PRD R-4\n(runtime-neutral content) and the R-4 acceptance test (grep for DDx-specific\ncommands in skill body must return zero hits). A Claude Code user or Genie user\nhas no `execute-bead` history to preserve.\n\n**Fix:** Replace with runtime-neutral language such as \"preserve the commit\nhistory from managed execution runs produced by the runtime.\" Add a per-runtime\npackaging note in `docs/install/ddx.md` for the DDx-specific commit-message\nconvention.\n\n### LEAK-2 — Skill body gates review filing on DDx/HELIX tracking\n\n`skills/helix/SKILL.md:173`\n\n```\nFile durable follow-up work for actionable medium-or-higher findings when\nthe project uses DDx/HELIX tracking.\n```\n\nThe `review` workflow contract conditions follow-up work on runtime presence\n(\"when the project uses DDx/HELIX tracking\"). The skill should produce follow-up\nwork unconditionally and let the runtime decide how to surface it.\n\n**Fix:** Remove the conditional. The skill should always produce follow-up\nwork items; whether they land as DDx beads, GitHub issues, or markdown stubs\nis a runtime concern.\n\n### LEAK-3 — Skill body references bead-first rules as DDx-specific\n\n`skills/helix/SKILL.md:275`\n\n```\nFor DDx-backed projects, obey bead-first rules before writing files or tracker\nmutations.\n```\n\nThe operating-discipline section names DDx explicitly in the normative body.\nBead-first discipline is a good general rule for any work-item–backed runtime.\nThe sentence should either be made runtime-neutral or moved to the DDx install\nguide.\n\n**Fix:** Either generalize (\"if the project uses a work-item tracker, check\nopen work items before writing files\") or move to `docs/install/ddx.md`.\n\n### LEAK-4 — Artifact schema directory layout shows DDx plugin path as catalog location\n\n`workflows/artifact-schema.md:279`\n\n```\nThe installed catalog lives under `.ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/`, and project\ninstances conventionally live under `docs/helix/`.\n```\n"}}

Prompt and workflow strategy\nHELIX owns:\n- prompt design\n- prompt engineering strategy\n- stage-authored behavior stance for planning, execution, review, alignment,\n  and related workflow stages\n- bead prompt structure\n- workflow wording and intervention policy\n- execution-document authoring conventions for HELIX artifacts (see [CONTRACT-002](CONTRACT-002-helix-execution-doc-conventions.md))\n\nHELIX does **not** expose stage personalities as a separate first-class\nworkflow configuration surface. The simpler contract is:\n\n- HELIX bakes stage stance into the governing action prompt, skill wording, or\n  execution-doc convention for that stage\n- DDx still owns harness/model execution and any concrete model resolution\n- if a stage needs a smarter, cheaper, slower, or faster lane, HELIX may ask\n  for tier or harness constraints, but it must not turn stage stance into\n  concrete model policy\n\nThe default HELIX stage stances are:\n\n| Stage family | Stance owned by HELIX | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Planning (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`, `triage`, `polish`) | exploratory, assumption-surfacing, artifact-authoring | widen context, expose ambiguity, prefer reversible shaping |\n| Managed execution (`build`, `measure`, execution-ready bead work) | contract-following, bounded, anti-feature-creep | implement only the governed slice and prove it deterministically |\n| Review (`review`, fresh-eyes passes) | adversarial, defect-seeking, risk-first | findings first; preserve merge/preserve evidence boundary |\n| Alignment (`align`) | top-down, conservative, drift-seeking | compare lower layers against higher-authority artifacts |\n| Supervisory/mechanical (`check`, `report`, queue steering) | concise, state-oriented, policy-applying | route work without inventing new product behavior |\n\nThese stances apply whether HELIX launches work through DDx-managed execution\nor through a direct non-managed prompt such as `ddx agent run` for planning,\nreview, or alignment. The stage selects the stance; DDx resolves the execution\nvehicle and concrete model policy.\n\n## Shared Integration Objects\n\nThese objects are shared across the boundary and should keep stable meanings.\n\n| Object | DDx role | HELIX role |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Bead** | tracker record + execution target | intended workspace-state transform (`B : W -\u003e W'`) |\n| **Workspace state** | execution substrate input/output surface | governed current/successor state under workflow interpretation |\n| **State delta** | realized material diff between workspace states | workflow-visible change to inspect during measure/report |\n| **Graph artifact** | indexed document node | governance/context layer |\n| **Execution doc** | discovered executable validation definition | authored validation contract |\n| **Execution run** | immutable evidence record | workflow input for measure/report/iteration |\n| **Metric** | structured runtime observation | workflow signal |\n| **Ratchet** | threshold/baseline evaluation | policy input for workflow decisions |\n| **Preserved attempt** | non-landed managed execution result | experiment/review/iteration input |\n\n## Workflow Handoff Points\n\n### HELIX -\u003e DDx\nHELIX decides:\n- what autonomy behavior should apply\n- what workflow prompt/context to use\n- what stage-authored stance should apply for the selected workflow step\n"}}

data.text: The current tree has already removed the old wrapper script and CLI harness files, but the execution contract still carries legacy model override language. I’m going to make the contract explicit in the DDx boundary doc and the execution guide: HELIX chooses stage tier intent, DDx/Fizeau resolves concrete routes, and legacy model pins are passthrough compatibility only.

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

• Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
• Maintain consistent development practices
• Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
• Install personas, prompts, and templates from the library

Get started:
  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues
  ddx cleanup       Inspect and clean stale DDx execution resources

More information:
  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  ac          Run pre-merge checks (declared in .ddx/checks/*.yaml)
  artifact    Inspect and regenerate DDx artifacts
  bead        Manage beads (portable work items)
  checkpoint  Create, list, or restore named git checkpoints
  cleanup     Inspect and clean stale DDx execution resources
  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project
  install     Install a package or resource
  installed   List installed packages
  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources
  log         Show DDX asset history
  metric      Inspect and run metric artifacts
  metrics     Show process metrics derived from beads and agent sessions
  outdated    List installed packages with available updates
  persona     Manage AI personas for consistent interactions
  plugin      Manage DDx plugins
  prompts     Manage AI prompts
  run         Invoke a task dispatcher for a prompt (layer-1 Fizeau Execute call)
  search      Search available packages
  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server
  skills      Validate bundled skill packages
  status      Show version and status information
  sync        Synchronize DDx-managed files with origin
  try         Execute a specific bead through the production machinery
  uninstall   Remove an installed package
  update      Update shipped skills and installed plugins
  upgrade     Upgrade DDx to the latest version
  verify      Verify installed package integrity
  version     Show version information
  work        Drain the bead execution queue

Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
  -h, --help                       help for ddx
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output
      --version                    version for ddx

Use "ddx [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Cancel received, shutting down gracefully
run dispatches a single task to the upstream agent service via one Fizeau
Execute call. It is the layer-1 primitive over which ddx try and ddx work add
bead lifecycle, queue semantics, and retry policy.

DDx passes all routing knobs (--harness, --provider, --model,
--min-power, --max-power, --profile) directly to the service without validation
or pre-resolution. The service owns route selection within those bounds
(CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).

Persona injection: --persona <name> loads the named persona and prepends its
body to the prompt as a system-prompt addendum before dispatch.

Examples:
  ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
  ddx run --min-power 10 --max-power 20 --prompt task.md
  ddx run --harness codex --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
  ddx run --provider openrouter --model qwen3.6-27b --prompt task.md
  ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
  ddx run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx run --text "explain this function"

Usage:
  ddx run [flags]

Flags:
      --effort string        Reasoning effort override (e.g. low, medium, high)
      --harness string       Harness constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)
  -h, --help                 help for run
      --json                 Output as JSON (alias for --output json-result)
      --max-power int        Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)
      --min-power int        Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)
      --model string         Model constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)
      --output string        Output format: text (default), json-result, session-jsonl (default "text")
      --permissions string   Permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
      --persona string       Persona name; body is prepended to the prompt as a system-prompt addendum
      --profile string       Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
      --project string       Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT
      --prompt string        Path to prompt file
      --provider string      Provider constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)
      --text string          Inline prompt text
      --timeout string       Request timeout (e.g. 30s, 5m)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Cancel received, shutting down gracefully
work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.

ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
bounds.

Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
label; otherwise the label is ignored.

Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
  drained     — no ready beads remain
  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts

work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.

Usage:
  ddx work [flags]
  ddx work [command]

Examples:
  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --once

  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
  ddx work --watch

  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s

  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing

  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90

Available Commands:
  analyze         Query cross-attempt performance from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
  clear-cooldowns Bulk-clear queue-drain cooldowns
  focus           Show work requiring operator attention
  metrics         Metrics over attempt evidence
  plan            Preview what 'ddx work' would pick (dry-run)
  status          Show live ddx workers scoped to this project

Flags:
      --effort string                    Effort level
      --from string                      Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)
      --harness string                   Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)
  -h, --help                             help for work
      --idle-interval duration           Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s)
      --json                             Output loop result as JSON
      --max-bead-cost float              Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label (default 5)
      --max-cost float                   Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited (default 100)
      --max-power int                    Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged
      --max-recovery-cost float          Per-bead automated recovery budget in USD for reframe/decompose attempts after repeated ladder exhaustion (default 2)
      --min-power int                    Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged
      --model string                     Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)
      --no-review                        Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)
      --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing   Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review
      --once                             Process at most one ready bead
      --preclaim-timeout duration        Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks (default 5m0s)
      --profile string                   Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)
      --project string                   Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT
      --provider string                  Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)
      --rate-limit-max-wait duration     Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry. (default 5m0s)
      --request-timeout duration         Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults
      --review-harness string            Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)
      --review-model string              Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)
      --watch                            Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx work [command] --help" for more information about a command.

try executes a single named bead through the full production
execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.

Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
    in dep order with an explicit ID)
  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time

Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers

Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
label; otherwise the label is ignored.

Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.

Exit codes:
  0  success or already_satisfied
  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success

Usage:
  ddx try <bead-id> [flags]

Examples:
  # Execute a specific bead
  ddx try ddx-abc12345

  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini

  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing

Flags:
      --effort string                    Effort level
      --from string                      Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)
      --harness string                   Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)
  -h, --help                             help for try
      --max-power int                    Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)
      --min-power int                    Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)
      --model string                     Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)
      --no-review                        Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)
      --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing   Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review
      --preclaim-timeout duration        Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks (default 5m0s)
      --profile string                   Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)
      --project string                   Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT
      --provider string                  Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)
      --request-timeout duration         Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults
      --review-harness string            Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)
      --review-model string              Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

+++ b/docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-ddx-helix-boundary.md
@@ -165,6 +165,48 @@ or through a direct non-managed prompt such as `ddx agent run` for planning,
 review, or alignment. The stage selects the stance; DDx resolves the execution
 vehicle and concrete model policy.
 
+### 6. Stage tier and model-policy handoff
+
+HELIX owns the stage-policy decision, not the model catalog. When a HELIX
+runtime adapter needs to express model strength, speed, or cost preference, it
+passes a structured routing request to DDx instead of resolving a provider model
+itself.
+
+The DDx adapter contract is:
+
+- HELIX may pass a DDx routing profile such as `smart`, `fast`, `cheap`, or
+  `default` for the selected stage.
+- HELIX may pass an operator-supplied harness or provider constraint when the
+  user intentionally chooses a runtime family.
+- HELIX may pass numeric power bounds only when the user or bead explicitly
+  supplies them; it should not convert its own stage stance into local numeric
+  model policy.
+- HELIX must not embed provider-specific model version strings as defaults,
+  fallback chains, or stage tables.
+- DDx forwards the routing request to its agent service; the agent service,
+  backed by the shared ddx-agent model catalog, resolves the concrete provider
+  and model under those constraints.
+- DDx records the resolved harness, provider, model, profile or power bounds,
+  and any fallback route in execution evidence so HELIX can measure outcomes
+  without owning model selection.
+
+Default HELIX stage tier intent:
+
+| Stage family | HELIX routing intent | DDx request |
+|---|---|---|
+| Planning and design (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`) | broad-context reasoning | `--profile smart` unless the operator provides a stronger constraint |
+| Alignment and review (`align`, `review`) | adversarial or drift-seeking reasoning | `--profile smart`; review may also use an alternate harness constraint for cross-harness verification |
+| Managed execution (`build`, `measure`, execution-ready bead work) | bounded implementation under bead acceptance | no local model default; delegate to DDx queue policy, operator constraints, and bead-specific constraints |
+| Supervisory and mechanical routing (`check`, `report`) | concise state classification | `--profile cheap` or `--profile fast` depending on whether cost or latency is the local operator preference |
+| Issue refinement (`polish`, decomposition support) | cheap by default, smart when ambiguity or cross-artifact design risk is detected | start with `--profile cheap`; escalate by explicit DDx retry/power policy, not HELIX hardcoded models |
+
+Exact model names are compatibility constraints, not HELIX policy. If an
+operator supplies an exact model through an environment variable or CLI flag,
+HELIX may forward that string as `--model` without parsing or validating it.
+The exact pin supersedes the stage profile for that dispatch because the user
+has intentionally constrained the route. HELIX still must not use that value as
+a fallback source for other stages.
+
 ## Shared Integration Objects
 
 These objects are shared across the boundary and should keep stable meanings.

index 77a70db..1a5ea59 100644
--- a/workflows/EXECUTION.md
+++ b/workflows/EXECUTION.md
@@ -504,17 +504,101 @@ helix align auth
 helix design auth
 ```
 
+## Model Routing Contract
+
+HELIX's DDx adapter selects the workflow stage and the routing intent for that
+stage. It does not select concrete provider model versions.
+
+When a compatibility wrapper dispatches planning, review, alignment, build, or
+queue-steering work to DDx, it should build a routing request from three
+separate inputs:
+
+| Input | Owner | Examples |
+|---|---|---|
+| Stage tier intent | HELIX | `smart` for design/review/alignment, `cheap` or `fast` for `check`/`report`, DDx default for ordinary managed build work |
+| Runtime-family constraint | Operator / HELIX wrapper | a selected harness, review harness, provider, or profile flag |
+| Concrete model resolution | DDx agent service | provider/model selected from ddx-agent catalog data under the requested profile, power bounds, and user constraints |
+
+The wrapper should express stage tier intent with DDx routing profiles
+(`--profile smart`, `--profile fast`, `--profile cheap`, or no profile for
+DDx default policy). It may also pass `--harness`, `--provider`,
+`--min-power`, or `--max-power` when those values come from an operator flag,
+environment variable, or bead-specific requirement. It must not translate
+`smart` or `cheap` into a provider-specific model name inside HELIX.
+
+Exact model strings remain supported only as compatibility pins. If an
+operator sets a legacy HELIX model override or passes a legacy model flag, the
+wrapper forwards that exact value to DDx as `--model` and omits the stage
+profile for that dispatch. DDx then treats the model as an opaque user
+constraint and the agent-service catalog still owns validation, fallback, and
+route evidence. HELIX must not parse the model string, infer provider family
+from it, or use it as a fallback for other stages.
+
+Compatibility rules:
+
+- `HELIX_MODEL` is a global exact-model pin for compatibility wrappers. It
+  supersedes stage profiles for all wrapper-launched dispatches.
+- `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` and legacy `--smart-model <model>` are deprecated exact
+  pins for stages whose HELIX intent is `smart`. They should produce a warning
+  that `--profile smart` or DDx profile configuration is preferred.
+- `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`, and legacy
+  `--cheap-model <model>` are deprecated exact pins for mechanical or
+  refinement stages. They should not define default cheap models.
+- `HELIX_AGENT`, `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT`, and corresponding harness flags are
+  harness-family constraints, not model policy. They may be forwarded as
+  `--harness` while DDx resolves the concrete model.
+- If no exact model pin exists, wrapper dry-run and help output must show tier
+  or profile intent, never a provider-specific model version.
+
+Implementation plan for the legacy `scripts/helix` wrapper:
+
+1. Replace any `resolve_model()` table of concrete provider models with a
+   `stage_route_request(stage)` helper that returns `profile`, optional
+   `harness` or `provider`, optional power bounds, and optional exact
+   `model_pin`.
+2. Map stage families to tier intent in one place: `design`/`review`/`align`
+   use `smart`, `check`/`report` use `cheap` or `fast`, `polish` starts with
+   `cheap`, and managed `build` work leaves model selection to DDx unless the
+   operator supplied a constraint.
+3. Assemble DDx commands from that structured request: pass `--profile` for
+   tier intent, pass `--model` only for explicit compatibility pins, and never
+   synthesize a concrete model string in HELIX.
+4. Keep legacy environment variables and flags as aliases that set
+   `model_pin`; emit a deprecation warning and preserve exact passthrough
+   behavior for existing operators.
+5. Remove concrete model defaults from wrapper help, dry-run examples, and
+   tests. Help should describe tiers/profiles and exact-model pins as
+   compatibility overrides.
+
+Test plan for the legacy `tests/helix-cli.sh` harness:
+
+- Add dry-run coverage proving `design`, `review`, and `align` dispatch with
+  `--profile smart` and without `--model` when no exact override is set.
+- Add dry-run coverage proving `check`/`report` and cheap refinement paths
+  dispatch with cheap or fast profile intent and without a hardcoded model.
+- Add coverage proving ordinary managed build dispatch leaves model selection
+  to DDx unless an operator constraint is present.
+- Add compatibility cases for `HELIX_MODEL`, `HELIX_SMART_MODEL`,
+  `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`,
+  `--smart-model`, and `--cheap-model`: each forwards the exact supplied value
+  as `--model`, omits the stage profile for that dispatch, and emits the
+  expected deprecation warning where applicable.
+- Add a negative guard that fails if wrapper output or tests reintroduce
+  provider-specific model version defaults in HELIX-owned policy.
+
 ## Environment Variables
 
 | Variable | Default | Description |
 |----------|---------|-------------|
 | `HELIX_AGENT` | `codex` | Default agent (`codex` or `claude`) |
 | `HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT` | `2700` | Agent timeout in seconds (45 minutes) |
-| `HELIX_MODEL` | — | Override AI model name (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6`) |
+| `HELIX_MODEL` | — | Legacy exact-model pin forwarded to DDx as `--model`; supersedes stage profiles |
 | `HELIX_EFFORT` | — | Set reasoning effort level for Claude/Codex |
 | `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT` | auto | Agent for cross-model reviews (defaults to the other agent) |
-| `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL` | — | Cheaper model for queue-drain decisions |
-| `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL` | — | Cheaper model for issue refinement |
+| `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `smart` tier intent |
+| `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `cheap` or `fast` tier intent |
+| `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for queue-drain decisions |
+| `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for issue refinement |
 | `HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT` | `<repo>/workflows` | Override the workflow library root |
 | `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` | `<repo>/.ddx` | Override the tracker directory |
 | `HELIX_BEADS_DIR` | `.beads` | Override the beads interop directory |

@@ -165,6 +165,48 @@ or through a direct non-managed prompt such as `ddx agent run` for planning,
 review, or alignment. The stage selects the stance; DDx resolves the execution
 vehicle and concrete model policy.
 
+### 6. Stage tier and model-policy handoff
+
+HELIX owns the stage-policy decision, not the model catalog. When a HELIX
+runtime adapter needs to express model strength, speed, or cost preference, it
+passes a structured routing request to DDx instead of resolving a provider model
+itself.
+
+The DDx adapter contract is:
+
+- HELIX may pass a DDx routing profile such as `smart`, `fast`, `cheap`, or
+  `default` for the selected stage.
+- HELIX may pass an operator-supplied harness or provider constraint when the
+  user intentionally chooses a runtime family.
+- HELIX may pass numeric power bounds only when the user or bead explicitly
+  supplies them; it should not convert its own stage stance into local numeric
+  model policy.
+- HELIX must not embed provider-specific model version strings as defaults,
+  fallback chains, or stage tables.
+- DDx forwards the routing request to its agent service; the agent service,
+  backed by the shared ddx-agent model catalog, resolves the concrete provider
+  and model under those constraints.
+- DDx records the resolved harness, provider, model, profile or power bounds,
+  and any fallback route in execution evidence so HELIX can measure outcomes
+  without owning model selection.
+
+Default HELIX stage tier intent:
+
+| Stage family | HELIX routing intent | DDx request |
+|---|---|---|
+| Planning and design (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`) | broad-context reasoning | `--profile smart` unless the operator provides a stronger constraint |
+| Alignment and review (`align`, `review`) | adversarial or drift-seeking reasoning | `--profile smart`; review may also use an alternate harness constraint for cross-harness verification |
+| Managed execution (`build`, `measure`, execution-ready bead work) | bounded implementation under bead acceptance | no local model default; delegate to DDx queue policy, operator constraints, and bead-specific constraints |
+| Supervisory and mechanical routing (`check`, `report`) | concise state classification | `--profile cheap` or `--profile fast` depending on whether cost or latency is the local operator preference |
+| Issue refinement (`polish`, decomposition support) | cheap by default, smart when ambiguity or cross-artifact design risk is detected | start with `--profile cheap`; escalate by explicit DDx retry/power policy, not HELIX hardcoded models |
+
+Exact model names are compatibility constraints, not HELIX policy. If an
+operator supplies an exact model through an environment variable or CLI flag,
+HELIX may forward that string as `--model` without parsing or validating it.
+The exact pin supersedes the stage profile for that dispatch because the user
+has intentionally constrained the route. HELIX still must not use that value as
+a fallback source for other stages.
+
 ## Shared Integration Objects
 
 These objects are shared across the boundary and should keep stable meanings.

+++ b/workflows/EXECUTION.md
@@ -504,17 +504,101 @@ helix align auth
 helix design auth
 ```
 
+## Model Routing Contract
+
+HELIX's DDx adapter selects the workflow stage and the routing intent for that
+stage. It does not select concrete provider model versions.
+
+When a compatibility wrapper dispatches planning, review, alignment, build, or
+queue-steering work to DDx, it should build a routing request from three
+separate inputs:
+
+| Input | Owner | Examples |
+|---|---|---|
+| Stage tier intent | HELIX | `smart` for design/review/alignment, `cheap` or `fast` for `check`/`report`, DDx default for ordinary managed build work |
+| Runtime-family constraint | Operator / HELIX wrapper | a selected harness, review harness, provider, or profile flag |
+| Concrete model resolution | DDx agent service | provider/model selected from ddx-agent catalog data under the requested profile, power bounds, and user constraints |
+
+The wrapper should express stage tier intent with DDx routing profiles
+(`--profile smart`, `--profile fast`, `--profile cheap`, or no profile for
+DDx default policy). It may also pass `--harness`, `--provider`,
+`--min-power`, or `--max-power` when those values come from an operator flag,
+environment variable, or bead-specific requirement. It must not translate
+`smart` or `cheap` into a provider-specific model name inside HELIX.
+
+Exact model strings remain supported only as compatibility pins. If an
+operator sets a legacy HELIX model override or passes a legacy model flag, the
+wrapper forwards that exact value to DDx as `--model` and omits the stage
+profile for that dispatch. DDx then treats the model as an opaque user
+constraint and the agent-service catalog still owns validation, fallback, and
+route evidence. HELIX must not parse the model string, infer provider family
+from it, or use it as a fallback for other stages.
+
+Compatibility rules:
+
+- `HELIX_MODEL` is a global exact-model pin for compatibility wrappers. It
+  supersedes stage profiles for all wrapper-launched dispatches.
+- `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` and legacy `--smart-model <model>` are deprecated exact
+  pins for stages whose HELIX intent is `smart`. They should produce a warning
+  that `--profile smart` or DDx profile configuration is preferred.
+- `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`, and legacy
+  `--cheap-model <model>` are deprecated exact pins for mechanical or
+  refinement stages. They should not define default cheap models.
+- `HELIX_AGENT`, `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT`, and corresponding harness flags are
+  harness-family constraints, not model policy. They may be forwarded as
+  `--harness` while DDx resolves the concrete model.
+- If no exact model pin exists, wrapper dry-run and help output must show tier
+  or profile intent, never a provider-specific model version.
+
+Implementation plan for the legacy `scripts/helix` wrapper:
+
+1. Replace any `resolve_model()` table of concrete provider models with a
+   `stage_route_request(stage)` helper that returns `profile`, optional
+   `harness` or `provider`, optional power bounds, and optional exact
+   `model_pin`.
+2. Map stage families to tier intent in one place: `design`/`review`/`align`
+   use `smart`, `check`/`report` use `cheap` or `fast`, `polish` starts with
+   `cheap`, and managed `build` work leaves model selection to DDx unless the
+   operator supplied a constraint.
+3. Assemble DDx commands from that structured request: pass `--profile` for
+   tier intent, pass `--model` only for explicit compatibility pins, and never
+   synthesize a concrete model string in HELIX.
+4. Keep legacy environment variables and flags as aliases that set
+   `model_pin`; emit a deprecation warning and preserve exact passthrough
+   behavior for existing operators.
+5. Remove concrete model defaults from wrapper help, dry-run examples, and
+   tests. Help should describe tiers/profiles and exact-model pins as
+   compatibility overrides.
+
+Test plan for the legacy `tests/helix-cli.sh` harness:
+
+- Add dry-run coverage proving `design`, `review`, and `align` dispatch with
+  `--profile smart` and without `--model` when no exact override is set.
+- Add dry-run coverage proving `check`/`report` and cheap refinement paths
+  dispatch with cheap or fast profile intent and without a hardcoded model.
+- Add coverage proving ordinary managed build dispatch leaves model selection
+  to DDx unless an operator constraint is present.
+- Add compatibility cases for `HELIX_MODEL`, `HELIX_SMART_MODEL`,
+  `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`,
+  `--smart-model`, and `--cheap-model`: each forwards the exact supplied value
+  as `--model`, omits the stage profile for that dispatch, and emits the
+  expected deprecation warning where applicable.
+- Add a negative guard that fails if wrapper output or tests reintroduce
+  provider-specific model version defaults in HELIX-owned policy.
+
 ## Environment Variables
 
 | Variable | Default | Description |
 |----------|---------|-------------|
 | `HELIX_AGENT` | `codex` | Default agent (`codex` or `claude`) |
 | `HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT` | `2700` | Agent timeout in seconds (45 minutes) |
-| `HELIX_MODEL` | — | Override AI model name (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6`) |
+| `HELIX_MODEL` | — | Legacy exact-model pin forwarded to DDx as `--model`; supersedes stage profiles |
 | `HELIX_EFFORT` | — | Set reasoning effort level for Claude/Codex |
 | `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT` | auto | Agent for cross-model reviews (defaults to the other agent) |
-| `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL` | — | Cheaper model for queue-drain decisions |
-| `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL` | — | Cheaper model for issue refinement |
+| `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `smart` tier intent |
+| `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `cheap` or `fast` tier intent |
+| `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for queue-drain decisions |
+| `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for issue refinement |
 | `HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT` | `<repo>/workflows` | Override the workflow library root |
 | `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` | `<repo>/.ddx` | Override the tracker directory |
 | `HELIX_BEADS_DIR` | `.beads` | Override the beads interop directory |

504	helix design auth
   505	```
   506	
   507	## Model Routing Contract
   508	
   509	HELIX's DDx adapter selects the workflow stage and the routing intent for that
   510	stage. It does not select concrete provider model versions.
   511	
   512	When a compatibility wrapper dispatches planning, review, alignment, build, or
   513	queue-steering work to DDx, it should build a routing request from three
   514	separate inputs:
   515	
   516	| Input | Owner | Examples |
   517	|---|---|---|
   518	| Stage tier intent | HELIX | `smart` for design/review/alignment, `cheap` or `fast` for `check`/`report`, DDx default for ordinary managed build work |
   519	| Runtime-family constraint | Operator / HELIX wrapper | a selected harness, review harness, provider, or profile flag |
   520	| Concrete model resolution | DDx agent service | provider/model selected from ddx-agent catalog data under the requested profile, power bounds, and user constraints |
   521	
   522	The wrapper should express stage tier intent with DDx routing profiles
   523	(`--profile smart`, `--profile fast`, `--profile cheap`, or no profile for
   524	DDx default policy). It may also pass `--harness`, `--provider`,
   525	`--min-power`, or `--max-power` when those values come from an operator flag,
   526	environment variable, or bead-specific requirement. It must not translate
   527	`smart` or `cheap` into a provider-specific model name inside HELIX.
   528	
   529	Exact model strings remain supported only as compatibility pins. If an
   530	operator sets a legacy HELIX model override or passes a legacy model flag, the
   531	wrapper forwards that exact value to DDx as `--model` and omits the stage
   532	profile for that dispatch. DDx then treats the model as an opaque user
   533	constraint and the agent-service catalog still owns validation, fallback, and
   534	route evidence. HELIX must not parse the model string, infer provider family
   535	from it, or use it as a fallback for other stages.
   536	
   537	Compatibility rules:
   538	
   539	- `HELIX_MODEL` is a global exact-model pin for compatibility wrappers. It
   540	  supersedes stage profiles for all wrapper-launched dispatches.
   541	- `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` and legacy `--smart-model <model>` are deprecated exact
   542	  pins for stages whose HELIX intent is `smart`. They should produce a warning
   543	  that `--profile smart` or DDx profile configuration is preferred.
   544	- `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`, and legacy
   545	  `--cheap-model <model>` are deprecated exact pins for mechanical or
   546	  refinement stages. They should not define default cheap models.
   547	- `HELIX_AGENT`, `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT`, and corresponding harness flags are
   548	  harness-family constraints, not model policy. They may be forwarded as
   549	  `--harness` while DDx resolves the concrete model.
   550	- If no exact model pin exists, wrapper dry-run and help output must show tier
   551	  or profile intent, never a provider-specific model version.
   552	
   553	Implementation plan for the legacy `scripts/helix` wrapper:
   554	
   555	1. Replace any `resolve_model()` table of concrete provider models with a
   556	   `stage_route_request(stage)` helper that returns `profile`, optional
   557	   `harness` or `provider`, optional power bounds, and optional exact
   558	   `model_pin`.
   559	2. Map stage families to tier intent in one place: `design`/`review`/`align`
   560	   use `smart`, `check`/`report` use `cheap` or `fast`, `polish` starts with
   561	   `cheap`, and managed `build` work leaves model selection to DDx unless the
   562	   operator supplied a constraint.
   563	3. Assemble DDx commands from that structured request: pass `--profile` for
   564	   tier intent, pass `--model` only for explicit compatibility pins, and never
   565	   synthesize a concrete model string in HELIX.
   566	4. Keep legacy environment variables and flags as aliases that set
   567	   `model_pin`; emit a deprecation warning and preserve exact passthrough
   568	   behavior for existing operators.
   569	5. Remove concrete model defaults from wrapper help, dry-run examples, and
   570	   tests. Help should describe tiers/profiles and exact-model pins as
   571	   compatibility overrides.
   572	
   573	Test plan for the legacy `tests/helix-cli.sh` harness:
   574	
   575	- Add dry-run coverage proving `design`, `review`, and `align` dispatch with
   576	  `--profile smart` and without `--model` when no exact override is set.
   577	- Add dry-run coverage proving `check`/`report` and cheap refinement paths
   578	  dispatch with cheap or fast profile intent and without a hardcoded model.
   579	- Add coverage proving ordinary managed build dispatch leaves model selection
   580	  to DDx unless an operator constraint is present.
   581	- Add compatibility cases for `HELIX_MODEL`, `HELIX_SMART_MODEL`,
   582	  `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL`, `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL`,
   583	  `--smart-model`, and `--cheap-model`: each forwards the exact supplied value
   584	  as `--model`, omits the stage profile for that dispatch, and emits the
   585	  expected deprecation warning where applicable.
   586	- Add a negative guard that fails if wrapper output or tests reintroduce
   587	  provider-specific model version defaults in HELIX-owned policy.
   588	
   589	## Environment Variables
   590	
   591	| Variable | Default | Description |
   592	|----------|---------|-------------|
   593	| `HELIX_AGENT` | `codex` | Default agent (`codex` or `claude`) |
   594	| `HELIX_AGENT_TIMEOUT` | `2700` | Agent timeout in seconds (45 minutes) |
   595	| `HELIX_MODEL` | — | Legacy exact-model pin forwarded to DDx as `--model`; supersedes stage profiles |
   596	| `HELIX_EFFORT` | — | Set reasoning effort level for Claude/Codex |
   597	| `HELIX_REVIEW_AGENT` | auto | Agent for cross-model reviews (defaults to the other agent) |
   598	| `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `smart` tier intent |
   599	| `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for stages with `cheap` or `fast` tier intent |
   600	| `HELIX_CHECK_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for queue-drain decisions |
   601	| `HELIX_POLISH_MODEL` | — | Deprecated exact-model pin for issue refinement |
   602	| `HELIX_LIBRARY_ROOT` | `<repo>/workflows` | Override the workflow library root |
   603	| `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` | `<repo>/.ddx` | Override the tracker directory |
   604	| `HELIX_BEADS_DIR` | `.beads` | Override the beads interop directory |
   605	| `HELIX_FORCE_EPHEMERAL` | `0` | Force ephemeral sessions (no resume) |
   606	| `HELIX_AUTO_ALIGN` | `1` | Enable auto-alignment on ALIGN/STOP |

160	| Alignment (`align`) | top-down, conservative, drift-seeking | compare lower layers against higher-authority artifacts |
   161	| Supervisory/mechanical (`check`, `report`, queue steering) | concise, state-oriented, policy-applying | route work without inventing new product behavior |
   162	
   163	These stances apply whether HELIX launches work through DDx-managed execution
   164	or through a direct non-managed prompt such as `ddx agent run` for planning,
   165	review, or alignment. The stage selects the stance; DDx resolves the execution
   166	vehicle and concrete model policy.
   167	
   168	### 6. Stage tier and model-policy handoff
   169	
   170	HELIX owns the stage-policy decision, not the model catalog. When a HELIX
   171	runtime adapter needs to express model strength, speed, or cost preference, it
   172	passes a structured routing request to DDx instead of resolving a provider model
   173	itself.
   174	
   175	The DDx adapter contract is:
   176	
   177	- HELIX may pass a DDx routing profile such as `smart`, `fast`, `cheap`, or
   178	  `default` for the selected stage.
   179	- HELIX may pass an operator-supplied harness or provider constraint when the
   180	  user intentionally chooses a runtime family.
   181	- HELIX may pass numeric power bounds only when the user or bead explicitly
   182	  supplies them; it should not convert its own stage stance into local numeric
   183	  model policy.
   184	- HELIX must not embed provider-specific model version strings as defaults,
   185	  fallback chains, or stage tables.
   186	- DDx forwards the routing request to its agent service; the agent service,
   187	  backed by the shared ddx-agent model catalog, resolves the concrete provider
   188	  and model under those constraints.
   189	- DDx records the resolved harness, provider, model, profile or power bounds,
   190	  and any fallback route in execution evidence so HELIX can measure outcomes
   191	  without owning model selection.
   192	
   193	Default HELIX stage tier intent:
   194	
   195	| Stage family | HELIX routing intent | DDx request |
   196	|---|---|---|
   197	| Planning and design (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`) | broad-context reasoning | `--profile smart` unless the operator provides a stronger constraint |
   198	| Alignment and review (`align`, `review`) | adversarial or drift-seeking reasoning | `--profile smart`; review may also use an alternate harness constraint for cross-harness verification |
   199	| Managed execution (`build`, `measure`, execution-ready bead work) | bounded implementation under bead acceptance | no local model default; delegate to DDx queue policy, operator constraints, and bead-specific constraints |
   200	| Supervisory and mechanical routing (`check`, `report`) | concise state classification | `--profile cheap` or `--profile fast` depending on whether cost or latency is the local operator preference |
   201	| Issue refinement (`polish`, decomposition support) | cheap by default, smart when ambiguity or cross-artifact design risk is detected | start with `--profile cheap`; escalate by explicit DDx retry/power policy, not HELIX hardcoded models |
   202	
   203	Exact model names are compatibility constraints, not HELIX policy. If an
   204	operator supplies an exact model through an environment variable or CLI flag,
   205	HELIX may forward that string as `--model` without parsing or validating it.
   206	The exact pin supersedes the stage profile for that dispatch because the user
   207	has intentionally constrained the route. HELIX still must not use that value as
   208	a fallback source for other stages.
   209	
   210	## Shared Integration Objects
   211	
   212	These objects are shared across the boundary and should keep stable meanings.
   213	
   214	| Object | DDx role | HELIX role |
   215	|---|---|---|
   216	| **Bead** | tracker record + execution target | intended workspace-state transform (`B : W -> W'`) |
   217	| **Workspace state** | execution substrate input/output surface | governed current/successor state under workflow interpretation |
   218	| **State delta** | realized material diff between workspace states | workflow-visible change to inspect during measure/report |
   219	| **Graph artifact** | indexed document node | governance/context layer |
   220	| **Execution doc** | discovered executable validation definition | authored validation contract |
